'''Gadwin Springer''' (born 4 April 1993) is a French professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and [[France national rugby league team|France]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] and the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. Springer spent time on loan from [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] at [[Halifax RLFC|Halifax]] and [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[2018 RFL Championship|Championship]]. He played for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in 2021 and signed for Toulouse in November 2021.<ref name="auto">{{Cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/2416/springer-returns-to-toulouse|title=Springer returns to Toulouse|website=www.superleague.co.uk|access-date=19 November 2021}}</ref> He was the first South American to ever appear in Super League.<ref name="auto1">{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/13995/9886755/gadwin-springer-has-joined-castleford-and-coach-daryl-powell-is-excited-about-the-signing|title=Gadwin Springer has joined Castleford and coach Daryl Powell is excited about the signing|website=Sky Sports|access-date=19 November 2021}}</ref>

==Background==
Springer was born in [[French Guiana]] and did not discover [[rugby league]] until he moved from South America to the south-west of France in 2005.<ref name="auto1"/> Springer originally moved to Villeneuve<ref>{{cite tweet|number=1456228559249461248|user=VilleneuveXIII|title=[COMMUNIQUÉ 🟢⚪️]#GoLéopards #OnNeLâcheRien|date=4 November 2021}}</ref> but developed his rugby in the [[Toulouse Olympique]] academy before being signed for Catalans Dragons.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/gadwin-springer-de-retour-au-to/|title=Gadwin SPRINGER de retour au TO|date=7 November 2021|access-date=19 November 2021}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Catalans Dragons===
Springer was signed by [[Catalans Dragons]] from the [[Toulouse Olympique]] academy and made his Super League début on 14 March 2014 as a substitute against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-catalans/teams/39887|title=Super League (Sky Sports)|website=SkySports|access-date=19 November 2021}}</ref>

===Castleford Tigers===
On 12 June 2015 Springer joined [[Castleford Tigers]] on a {{frac|2|1|2}}-year contract.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/33113563|title=Castleford sign French prop Springer|access-date=19 November 2021|work=www.bbc.co.uk|date=12 June 2015 }}</ref>

He was a member of Castleford's squad which won the [[League Leaders Shield]] in 2017, making 21 appearances during the season, and was named as 18th man for the [[2017 Super League Grand Final]].

===Halifax===
On 6 November 2020 it was announced that Springer would join [[Halifax Panthers]] for the 2021 season<ref>{{cite web|website=www.rugby-league.com|date=6 Nov 2020|access-date=26 Dec 2020|title=HALIFAX BRING IN GADWIN SPRINGER|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/57327/halifax-bring-in-gadwin-springer}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 26 July 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=26 Jul 2021|access-date=26 Jul 2021|title=Featherstone announce Gadwin Springer signing|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/featherstone-announce-gadwin-springer-signing/}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
In September 2021 it was reported that Springer would join [[Villeneuve XIII RLLG|Villeneuve Leopards]] in the French [[Elite One Championship]] at the end of his contract with Featherstone.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://treizemondial.fr/gadwin-springer-de-retour-en-elite-1/|title=Gadwin Springer de retour en Elite 1|date=12 September 2021|access-date=19 November 2021}}</ref> Subsequently [[Toulouse Olympique]] won promotion to Super League<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12430948/million-pound-game-toulouse-promoted-to-super-league-after-beating-featherstone|title=Million Pound Game: Toulouse promoted to Super League after beating Featherstone|website=Sky Sports|access-date=19 November 2021}}</ref> and on 3 November it was announced that Springer had signed for Toulouse for their 2022 Super League season.<ref name="auto"/>

===Featherstone Rovers (re-join)===
On 30 June 2022, Springer signed a contract to return to Featherstone ahead of the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/gadwin-springer-returns-featherstone-rovers/|title=Gadwin Springer returns to Featherstone after Toulouse exit|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=30 June 2022 }}</ref>

===Doncaster RLFC===
On 9 November 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Doncaster RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=9 Nov 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Veteran former Super League prop seals cross-Championship switch ahead of 2026|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/gadwin-springer-joins-doncaster-ahead-of-2026}}</ref>

===Hunslet RLFC (loan)===
On 19 March 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hunslet RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on one-week loan<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 Mar 2026|accessdate=20 Mar 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Championship club make trio of loan signings including former Super League pair|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/doncaster-trio-join-hunslet-on-loan-19-03-2026}}</ref>

==International career==
Gadwin was selected for [[France national rugby league team|France]] for their [[2015 European Cup]] campaign. He made his international début against Ireland in Albi. He also played for France in their mid-tournament test match against England. He was a part of what was considered a 'weakened' French side due to injury and it showed with an appalling showing against their opponents.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live/match/43791/report|title=England demolish France 84-4 in record win|publisher=Skysports.com|access-date=24 October 2015}}</ref> Springer played in France's lone international fixture of 2016, an [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|end of year test match]] against England in Avignon.{{citation needed|date=January 2024}}
